title: FDA grants priority review for iberdomide-based myeloma regimen
source_summary_bullets: FDA granted priority review for iberdomide, a first-in-class oral CELMoD designed to degrade transcription factors more effectively than earlier agents
Priority review supported by Phase III data showing iberdomide combinations significantly improve minimal residual disease
clinical_interpretation_bullets: Clinical context: Represents potential new treatment option for multiple myeloma patients, particularly relevant for hospitalists managing myeloma complications and coordinating care with hematology-oncology
bottom_line: FDA priority review of iberdomide offers potential new oral treatment option for multiple myeloma patients.

title: Placebo may be inferior compared to acetaminophen when given with morphine for the management of acute pain in the emergency department
source_summary_bullets: Morphine plus placebo is not noninferior to morphine plus acetaminophen for acute pain management in the emergency department
clinical_interpretation_bullets: Clinical context: Supports routine addition of acetaminophen to opioids for acute pain management, relevant for hospitalists managing pain in admitted patients and optimizing multimodal analgesia
limitations: Study limited to emergency department setting
bottom_line: Adding acetaminophen to morphine appears superior to morphine alone for acute pain management.

title: Higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden may predict postoperative cardiovascular complications and mortality in obstructive sleep apnea
source_summary_bullets: Higher sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden (SASHB) was associated with significantly greater risk of 30-day postoperative cardiovascular events and mortality in OSA patients
clinical_interpretation_bullets: Clinical context: May help hospitalists better risk-stratify OSA patients undergoing surgery and optimize perioperative monitoring and management strategies
limitations: SASHB measurement may not be readily available in all clinical settings
bottom_line: Sleep apnea-specific hypoxic burden may serve as a useful perioperative risk stratification tool for OSA patients.

title: Lifestyle factors including higher body mass index and smoking are associated with changes in left-atrial size and function
source_summary_bullets: Higher BMI and smoking are associated with measurable changes in left-atrial size and function
Left atrial size and function are strong predictors of atrial fibrillation
clinical_interpretation_bullets: Clinical context: Reinforces importance of lifestyle counseling for cardiovascular risk reduction and may help identify patients at higher risk for developing atrial fibrillation
bottom_line: Obesity and smoking cause measurable left atrial changes that may predispose to atrial fibrillation.
